Welcome back to the Ultimate NoBS Referral Machine. We're in session number three here. And in this session, Dan will reveal three key elements you need as part of your NoBS Referral Machine as the actual part of the machine, the client newsletter. We're also gonna talk about a powerful stealth way to get clients to refer even when they don't want to refer. So let's start, though, with testimonials. And why are testimonies important? Are they still important today? Well, it's specific to referral activity. They're important because I feel better referring people to you if there's a lot of social proof. Right, this is not just me referring. Well, exactly. Yeah. So in general, they are still the most believable kind of proof. So factual proof, scientific proof, even demonstration proof, you know, in infomercials, we always said that the easiest show to do is the show where the product is the star. So you can shove a whole ham and six vegetables and five bananas in the top of the blender and make something, right? However, even those, the public has a skepticism about the demonstration, right? Because you could rig the demonstration. And by the way, you can rig that demonstration. But real people telling their stories, it's the most believable kind of proof there is. It can be the most motivational kind of proof because I wanna be like this person and the result they got. I identify with this person, right? And it can be the most interesting because human interest stories are inherently more interesting than facts, figures, science, studies, et cetera. So, you know, when you said, are they still important today? That suggests a premise that, you know, this has worn out its welcome in the marketplace and nothing could be further from the truth. Now, specific to referrals, again, the more social proof you have and that you are visibly using, that I know the person I refer is going to encounter, than the less anxious I am about referring. It takes really the onus off of me at that point because now the person I've referred sees all of these other people and hears from all of these other people and is now really making a composite decision rather than a decision for which I have all the responsibility. Right, yeah, exactly. So, we know that they're important. What's the best way to collect them? How do I go about it? I'm shocked that most business owners don't have any. Point of a gun. Right. Look, a lot of business owners don't have them. Right. A lot of business owners have them that have accumulated accidentally and they've been using them. But hardly anybody really is prioritizing the collection of them on a day by day, week by week, customer by customer, patient by patient basis, which is what should be done. So, one good way to collect them is to ask for them. Right. And if you are unwilling to ask, then it makes it all the more important to have very visible use. So, the use itself is asking. Sure, right. This is what we do here. Yeah, exactly. So, if I come into your office, and as we talked about before, the walls are covered with fake monnaies, that doesn't communicate anything to me, except maybe you have good taste in art. If the walls are covered with photographs and testimonials, big blown up in frame, all the way down the hallway, I'm starting to get the message. Right. And so, the visible non-ask is important under any conditions, but it's even more important if you are absolutely gonna refuse to sit down face to face with a customer, client, or patient, an outright ask. Whatever you do, you have to have a system for this. So, if I am going to give you a testimonial, the worst thing you can do is, hey Dave, the next time you come in, if you write out and bring a testimony, well, you don't know how to do that. Right. And you don't know where you got to pen, and you don't have a pad, and you don't, I mean, on and on and on and on and on. I'm asking, you know, I'm asking- It's not gonna happen. I'm asking my dog to take a picture of himself, and you know, email me a selfie, you know? I mean, anybody water would accommodate me, but he doesn't have fingers. I mean, it's a-
